calculation.method: AFP close price and percentage change; the -9.99% figure is consistent with the -10.0% rounded close reported by AFP and with the intraday 9.1% decline reported by Bloomberg after the circuit breaker resumed

text: South Korea's KOSPI crashed 9.99% to close at 8,203.84 on 2026-06-23, its steepest one-day drop since early March, one session after the benchmark set a record high. AFP rounded the move to -10.0% at the close; Bloomberg reported the intraday decline had extended to 9.1% after the 20-minute circuit breaker resumed, before worsening into the close.

value.foreign_outflow: more than 4 trillion won of KOSPI shares sold by foreign investors
value.etf_amplification: leveraged ETFs tied to chipmakers amplified price swings; top financial watchdog expressed regret having allowed the risky products

text: The KOSPI crash was driven by its semiconductor heavyweights: SK Hynix slumped more than 12% and Samsung Electronics lost more than 10%, together accounting for the bulk of the index's decline. The Korea Exchange triggered a 20-minute circuit breaker suspension intraday. Foreign investors sold more than 4 trillion won of KOSPI shares while retail traders added positions; Bloomberg reported that leveraged ETFs tied to the chipmakers had amplified price swings, prompting the top financial watchdog to express regret for having allowed the risky products.

value.prior_close_note: both benchmarks pulled back from record highs set earlier in the week; the Nikkei 225 had closed at a record 72,353.96 on 2026-06-22

market_session: Tokyo Stock Exchange regular-session close on 2026-06-23

calculation.method: reported close and daily move from AFP and Trading Economics; daily moves are versus the 2026-06-22 regular-session close

text: Japanese benchmarks retreated from record highs. The Nikkei 225 fell 3.6% to close at 69,788.38 and the broader TOPIX dropped 2.6% to 3,990 on 2026-06-23, both pulling back from records set earlier in the week as investors took profits on semiconductor and AI-related names after the strong rally.

market_session: Tokyo Stock Exchange regular session on 2026-06-23
text: The Japanese decline was concentrated in technology and chip-related names. Losses were led by Kioxia, which slumped 15.1%, and SoftBank Group, which fell 10.1%. Chip-equipment maker Tokyo Electron shed 6.2% and PCB maker Ibiden dropped 6.6% per Trading Economics close-level readings for 2026-06-23. AFP separately reported SoftBank down more than 10% and Tokyo Electron 6.2% lower.

value.note: AFP reported Sydney, Singapore, Wellington, Mumbai, Bangkok and Jakarta sharply down; Reuters early-session intraday readings showed S&P/ASX 200 roughly flat, CSI 300 down about 1%, Shanghai Composite down about 0.3%, and Nifty up about 0.4% before regional selling intensified into the close

calculation.method: AFP close levels for Hang Seng and Shanghai Composite; Reuters/Investing.com intraday readings used as context for the early-session picture and are marked as intraday

text: The selloff was broad-based across the region. Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 2.1% to 23,263.41 and the Shanghai Composite dropped 1.4% to 4,106.25 per AFP close readings. AFP reported that Sydney, Singapore, Wellington, Mumbai, Bangkok and Jakarta were all sharply down. The Reuters early-session wrap, published before selling intensified, had shown the S&P/ASX 200 roughly flat and the Nifty slightly higher; those intraday readings are carried at medium confidence and superseded by the final regional picture.

subject.topic: AI/chip rally profit-taking and stretched valuations

value.theme: profit-taking on overheated AI and semiconductor trades after weeks of outsized gains
value.trigger_chain: Broadcom results reportedly failed to deliver expected guidance; Nasdaq fell overnight; selling rolled into Asia at the open and intensified through the session
value.spacex: SpaceX fell about 16% on 2026-06-23, reportedly losing roughly $600 billion in market value over three days, and disclosed plans for an inaugural bond offering
value.valuation_warning: IG's Tony Sycamore: valuations have become stretched and the bar is now materially higher; questions around AI capex and returns remain unanswered
value.hyundai_research: Joo Won of Hyundai Research Institute called the scale of the KOSPI decline excessive and warned more selling pressure may be in the wings
value.allspring: Gary Tan of Allspring Global Investments: moves amplified by liquidity and derivative positioning; Korean market increasingly sensitive to retail leverage

text: The regional selloff was a coordinated unwinding of crowded AI and semiconductor trades that had powered Asian benchmarks to record highs into June. AFP framed the trigger as doubts over the wisdom of the vast sums being pumped into AI, with Nvidia above $5 trillion in value and SpaceX plunging about 16% after its IPO; IG's Tony Sycamore warned that valuations had become stretched and capex returns remained unproven. Hyundai Research Institute's Joo Won called the KOSPI decline excessive but warned more selling pressure could follow. Allspring's Gary Tan said liquidity and derivative positioning were amplifying the moves, particularly given the leveraged retail base in Korea.

value.iran_context: US granted Tehran a 60-day license to sell oil on international markets; US VP Vance said a very good foundation was laid for negotiations; Strait of Hormuz reopening to tanker traffic
value.yen: yen sat just below a 40-year low against the dollar near 161.93; Japanese authorities spent more than $70 billion propping up the currency in the prior month; Finance Minister Katayama and US Treasury Secretary Bessent reportedly spoke about exchange rates
value.fed_context: the hawkish Federal Reserve meeting the prior week had prompted traders to scale back rate-cut expectations, amplifying pressure on regional risk assets

text: Oil extended losses on optimism over US-Iran peace talks, with WTI down 1.5% to $72.77 and Brent down 1.6% to $76.65 per AFP close readings. The US granted Tehran a 60-day license to sell oil internationally and both sides spoke of progress in Switzerland negotiations. The macro overhang for Asian risk assets remained: the prior week's hawkish Federal Reserve meeting had scaled back rate-cut expectations, the yen sat near a 40-year low close to 161.93 per dollar despite more than $70 billion of intervention the prior month, and Finance Minister Katayama and US Treasury Secretary Bessent had reportedly spoken about exchange rates.
